text:
HEMATOPATHOLOGY / BONE MARROW PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient Name: Margaret Chen
Date of Birth: 1977
Sex: Female
Medical Record Number: [on file]
Ordering Physician: Hematology/Oncology Attending, Allogeneic Transplant Program
Clinical History: 48-year-old female with AML, NOS (FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, DNMT3A R882H, RUNX1 p.S291fs, TET2 p.Q916*, normal karyotype; ELN 2022 Adverse risk). Treated with 7+3 induction chemotherapy (cytarabine/daunorubicin) plus midostaurin. Achieved morphologic CR with MRD negativity after cycle 1. Subsequently underwent myeloablative allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant (10/10 HLA-matched unrelated donor, peripheral blood stem cells, FluBu4 conditioning, tacrolimus/MTX GVHD prophylaxis). Day +30 assessment confirmed engraftment with full donor chimerism. Mild chronic GVHD (skin lichenoid pattern, GI) identified around day +100, managed with topical clobetasol, oral budesonide, and continuation of tacrolimus. This biopsy is the 6-month (day +180) post-transplant surveillance assessment.

Specimen ID: SP-XXXX-XXXX
Date of Procedure: [date entered by system]
Procedure Type: Bilateral posterior iliac crest bone marrow biopsy (trephine core) and aspirate
Site: Right and left posterior iliac crest
Received: Two trephine core biopsy specimens in formalin (right PICA labeled "right core," left PICA labeled "left core") and aspirate smears (10 air-dried Wright-Giemsa stained slides, 4 EDTA anticoagulated tubes for flow cytometry and molecular studies, 1 sodium heparin tube for cytogenetics and chimerism analysis)
Clinical Indication: Post-allogeneic stem cell transplant surveillance (day +180); assessment for disease recurrence, engraftment status, and chimerism

---

GROSS DESCRIPTION

Right posterior iliac crest trephine core biopsy: Received in formalin, a tan-pink cylindrical core measuring 1.7 cm in length x 0.3 cm in diameter. The specimen is intact, firm, and trabeculated on cross-section. Submitted in entirety in one cassette.

Left posterior iliac crest trephine core biopsy: Received in formalin, a tan-pink cylindrical core measuring 1.5 cm in length x 0.3 cm in diameter. The specimen is intact and trabeculated. Submitted in entirety in one cassette.

Aspiration smears: Ten air-dried Wright-Giemsa stained smears received; adequate spicules present with good cellularity on low-power scan. Submitted for differential count.

---

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION

Bone Marrow Biopsy (Trephine Cores - Bilateral):
The trephine core sections are well-fixed and processable. Sections are examined at multiple levels with hematoxylin and eosin (H&E) stain.

Cellularity: The marrow is normocellular, with an estimated overall cellularity of 45-55% (age-adjusted normal range for a 48-year-old: approximately 40-60%). The fat-to-hematopoietic ratio is within expected limits bilaterally. No focal hypercellular nodules or hypocellular areas are identified.

Architecture: Normal trabecular architecture is maintained. The interstitial space contains a mixture of maturing hematopoietic elements without architectural distortion.

Myeloid Series (Granulopoiesis): Myeloid maturation is present and orderly, proceeding through recognizable promyelocyte, myelocyte, metamyelocyte, band, and segmented neutrophil stages. Myeloblasts are not increased by morphologic assessment; blasts are estimated to account for <2% of total nucleated cells (confirmed on aspirate differential, see below). No clusters or sheets of blasts are identified. No Auer rods are visualized. No dysgranulopoietic features (e.g., pseudo-Pelger-Huet anomaly, hypogranular forms) are identified.

Erythroid Series (Erythropoiesis): Erythroid elements are present in adequate numbers with normal island formation. Maturation proceeds in an orderly fashion from proerythroblasts through orthochromic normoblasts. No significant dyserythropoiesis (e.g., nuclear budding, multinucleation, megaloblastoid changes) is appreciated.

Megakaryocytes: Megakaryocytes are present in adequate numbers, with approximately 3-6 per high-power field. Morphology is normal; forms show appropriate lobation and normal cytoplasmic granularity. No micromegakaryocytes, hypolobated or mononuclear megakaryocytes, or clustering abnormalities are identified.

Lymphoid Cells and Plasma Cells: Scattered lymphocytes and rare plasma cells are present in normal proportions. No lymphoid aggregates, paratrabecular lymphoid infiltrates, or expansile plasma cell aggregates are observed. No evidence of lymphoproliferative disorder.

Stromal Elements: No fibrosis identified on reticulin stain (MF-0 to MF-1, within normal limits for post-transplant setting). No necrosis. Sinusoidal architecture intact.

Bone Marrow Aspirate Differential Count (500-cell differential):
- Myeloblasts: 1.4%
- Promyelocytes: 4.6%
- Myelocytes: 11.8%
- Metamyelocytes: 9.2%
- Band forms: 14.0%
- Segmented neutrophils: 18.6%
- Eosinophil precursors and mature eosinophils: 2.4%
- Basophils: 0.2%
- Monocytes/promonocytes: 3.2%
- Proerythroblasts: 1.8%
- Basophilic normoblasts: 5.0%
- Polychromatophilic normoblasts: 12.6%
- Orthochromatic normoblasts: 8.8%
- Lymphocytes: 6.2%
- Plasma cells: 0.2%
M:E ratio: approximately 1.8:1 (within normal limits)

No blast excess. No Auer rods. No morphologically atypical cells identified. No leukoerythroblastic reaction.

---

ANCILLARY STUDIES

1. Immunohistochemistry (IHC) on paraffin sections:
   - CD34 (QBEnd/10): Scattered CD34-positive cells present, consistent with normal precursor population (<1% of marrow cells). No CD34-positive blast clusters or sheets.
   - CD117 (c-Kit): Rare mast cells and scattered progenitors present; no aberrant increase.
   - MPO (myeloperoxidase): Highlights normal myeloid maturation pattern.
   - CD3: Scattered T lymphocytes present in normal distribution; no expansile T-cell infiltrate.
   - CD20: Scattered B lymphocytes; no follicles or B-cell aggregates.
   - CD138: Rare plasma cells; no plasmacytosis.
   - TdT: Negative; no excess lymphoblasts.
   - Ki-67 (MIB-1): Proliferative index of hematopoietic precursors appropriate for active trilineage hematopoiesis.

2. Flow Cytometry Immunophenotyping for MRD (10-color panel, submitted from aspirate):
   Performed on CD45 vs. side scatter-gated marrow elements. A leukemia-associated immunophenotype (LAIP) panel targeting the previously characterized blast phenotype (CD34+/CD117+/CD13+/CD33+/MPO+/HLA-DR+/CD7 dim) was employed.
   Result: NO IMMUNOPHENOTYPICALLY ABERRANT BLAST POPULATION DETECTED. The percentage of CD34+/CD117+ blasts is 0.009%, below the threshold of detection (sensitivity limit 0.01%). No aberrant antigen co-expression pattern consistent with the index LAIP is identified. MRD by flow cytometry: NEGATIVE.

3. Cytogenetics (Conventional Karyotype from Aspirate):
   Specimen: Heparin anticoagulated bone marrow aspirate, cultured 24 and 48 hours.
   Result: 46,XY [20/20 metaphases analyzed]
   Interpretation: Normal male karyotype in all metaphases examined. This is consistent with complete donor engraftment (donor is a male; patient is female with original 46,XX karyotype). No cytogenetic abnormalities detected.
   Note: Loss of Y chromosome not identified. No del(5q), del(7q), trisomy 8, or other AML-associated cytogenetic changes detected.

4. Donor Chimerism Analysis (Short Tandem Repeat [STR] Polymorphism Analysis):
   Specimens submitted: Total nucleated cells (unsorted), immunomagnetically separated CD3+ (T-cell) fraction, and CD33+ (myeloid cell) fraction from aspirate.
   Method: Capillary electrophoresis of informative STR loci (donor vs. recipient polymorphisms previously established at time of engraftment assessment).
   Results:
   - Total nucleated cell chimerism: 99.5% donor / 0.5% recipient
   - CD33+ myeloid cell chimerism: 100% donor / 0% recipient
   - CD3+ T-cell chimerism: 98% donor / 2% recipient
   Interpretation: Full donor chimerism is maintained in the myeloid compartment. Near-complete donor chimerism in the T-cell compartment. No evidence of recipient hematopoietic reconstitution or mixed chimerism concerning for impending relapse.

5. Molecular Studies (in progress / results pending):
   a. FLT3-ITD by quantitative RT-PCR: RESULT PENDING (specimen forwarded to molecular pathology laboratory; expected result within 5 business days)
   b. DNMT3A p.R882H by droplet digital PCR (ddPCR): RESULT PENDING
   c. RUNX1 p.S291fs*16 by next-generation sequencing deep amplicon sequencing: RESULT PENDING
   Note: Results will be issued as an addendum to this report upon completion.

6. Reticulin Stain (Gordon-Sweets): Grade MF-0 to MF-1. Delicate reticulin fibers present in a normal distribution. No coarse reticulin fibrosis or collagen fibrosis (MF-2 or MF-3) identified.

---

DIAGNOSTIC SUMMARY

Bone marrow biopsy and aspirate (bilateral posterior iliac crest), day +180 post-allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant:

1. NORMOCELLULAR BONE MARROW WITH TRILINEAGE HEMATOPOIESIS, NO MORPHOLOGIC EVIDENCE OF ACUTE MYELOID LEUKEMIA.
   - Myeloblasts account for 1.4% of nucleated cells on aspirate differential (within normal limits; <5% threshold for morphologic complete remission).
   - No Auer rods identified.
   - No dysplastic features to suggest myelodysplastic syndrome or impending relapse.

2. COMPLETE DONOR CHIMERISM CONFIRMED BY STR ANALYSIS.
   - Total chimerism: 99.5% donor.
   - Myeloid (CD33+) chimerism: 100% donor.
   - T-cell (CD3+) chimerism: 98% donor.
   - Cytogenetics show 46,XY donor karyotype in all metaphases, corroborating full donor engraftment.

3. MRD NEGATIVE BY 10-COLOR FLOW CYTOMETRY (below 0.01% threshold).

4. MOLECULAR STUDIES (FLT3-ITD, DNMT3A R882H, RUNX1 frameshift) PENDING; ADDENDUM TO FOLLOW.

5. NO CYTOGENETIC ABNORMALITIES DETECTED (46,XY, normal donor karyotype).

---

ADDENDUM NOTE (ANTICIPATED):
Molecular PCR/ddPCR/NGS results for FLT3-ITD, DNMT3A p.R882H, and RUNX1 p.S291fs*16 are pending at the time of this report. An addendum will be issued with final molecular MRD results. Based on the flow cytometry negativity and complete donor chimerism, durable complete remission is favored pending molecular confirmation.

---

INTERPRETIVE COMMENT:
This bone marrow biopsy, performed at 6 months post-allogeneic stem cell transplant, demonstrates sustained morphologic and immunophenotypic remission. The finding of normocellular marrow with trilineage hematopoiesis, blast percentage within normal limits, MRD negativity by sensitive flow cytometry, and full donor chimerism by STR analysis is consistent with durable complete remission following allogeneic HSCT for AML. The near-complete T-cell chimerism (98% donor) is within the expected range for this timepoint and does not suggest clinically concerning mixed chimerism. These findings are reassuring and consistent with the expected post-transplant course in a patient who achieved MRD-negative CR prior to transplant with a fully matched unrelated donor graft.

Specimen adequacy: Adequate for all requested studies. Core biopsies of adequate length and processability.

Diagnosing Pathologist: [Attending Hematopathologist]
Transcribed and reviewed by: [Fellow, Hematopathology]
Date of final sign-out: [entered by system]
Addendum pending: Molecular MRD studies (FLT3-ITD qRT-PCR, DNMT3A ddPCR, RUNX1 deep NGS)
